text: Assistant: The implementation seam is clear: extend the canonical message read model with `reply_to`, then build one daemon wait primitive over that model. `channel send --wait` can publish first and safely wait afterward because the wait subscribes before backfill; a very fast reply is recovered from storage rather than missed. [...] This changes my earlier exit-code recommendation: because timeout is an expected agent outcome, it should exit `0`. Actual daemon, identity, or channel-resolution failures remain nonzero. `channel send --wait` should print no intermediate “sent” line—only the reply envelope or timeout envelope.
